PETER A. BAGATELOS
LAW OFFICES OF
BAGATELOS & FADEM
THE INTERNATIONAL BUILOING
60I CALIFORN IA STREET
suliE raor
5AN FRANCISCO. CALIFORNIA 94IOA
october 29, L99O
. 
; Ii 
!I 
TELEPHONE
..- lll -i 
(4rs, saz_7roo
FAX
(4r5) 942 - rOAS
Mr. Mark Morodomi
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 8o0
Sacramento, cA 
9 5814
Dear i,Ir. l,Iorodoini i
This will confirm our telephone conversation on october 15,
l-990. On that date, you provided rne with Legal advice in response
to facts $rhich I had previously presented to you in several prior
teLephone conversations. I sha1I briefly recite the basic facts
and then confirn the advice you gave me,
rf you recall, I initially 
called $rith a general inquiry
regarding this general subject matter area and merel-y requested
general guidance from you, ouring the course of several tefephone
conversations, you required rne to disclose the name of my client
and the specific background facts in order for you and your
colleagues to be able to provide rne with a response. While I found
this quite unusual, having never been required by the FPPC when I
have sought advice over the last fourteen years to provide details
regarding my client in order first to obtain advice, I nevertheless
received perrnission fron ny client to provide the information and
did so. At ny request, you did check r^/ith others in your office
and l-ater advised me that, despite your earlier requirement that I
provide particular facts to you, it was confirmed that I and others
tike me could still 
inquire and receive general advice and guidance
on an informal basis without disclosing particular facts.
I advised you that I am representing The conmj,ttee to Re-elect
BiII Honig, a conrnittee controlled by BilI Honig, Cafifornia
Superintendent of Public Instruction. I advised you that Mr. Honig
has been asked to attend events sponsored by the Friends of Langley
Porter Psychiatric Institute, 
Iocated at the University of
California San Francisco Med.icaI center and by the Exploratorium,
also located in san Franci-sco. Both of these entities 
are
charitable, non-profit organizations, exenpt from tax under Section
5o1(c) (3) of the Internal Revenue Code. We also discussed specific
dates. 
It 
is anticipated that Mr. Honig will 
receive some
recognition for his position at both events, which are to be held
very soon. I advised you that both entities provide proqrans that
are nrell within the broad scope of concern of the state educational
system .


I also described to you that Mr. Honj-g would like to rnake
gifts to his principal staff assistants in his state office during
the holiday season.
I ask for confirrnation as to whether Mr. Honig may use funds
from his re-election committee for the above purposes.
You advised me that, under the circumstances I described to
you, it is perrnissible for l,tr. Honig to use his carnpaign funds to
attend the Friends of Langley Porter Psychiatric Institute 
and
Exploratoriun events. You indicated that since these activities
are both charitable in nature and reasonably rel-ated to 
a
political, 
governmental, or legi.slative purpose, the expenditures
$/ould be permitted under the personal use statutes (covernment Code
Section 85800 et. seq) within the PoIiti-cal Reform Act as amended.
You further advised me that gifts fron campaign funds made to
employees or campaign corunittee workers, or to an employee of the
elected officer's 
agency, are considered under Government Code
S85802(f) (2) to be directly related to a political, 
legislative, or
governmental purpose if the gifts total less than $l-00 in a
calendar year to each such empJ-oyee. I asked for clarification 
as
to whether a gift of $1oo or more would be permitted. You advised
me in a telephone cal"l- on october 29t 1990, that covernment Code
S85802(f) (1) prohibits any such compensation to public employees,
with the exception of less than $1-00 gifts described above
Thank you very much for your advice. I have advised rny client
accordingly and, due to the timing of the impending events, Mr.
Honig may proceed to pay for such events in reliance on your
advice.
very trul-y yours,
?,**A
Peter A. Bagatel
PAB/cc
cc: Honorable Bi I1 Honig
honig: morodomi . ltr

sections.question_synthetic: A lawyer seeks confirmation of FPPC oral advice regarding whether campaign funds can be used for a candidate to attend charitable events and to give holiday gifts to state employees.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is incoming correspondence from a lawyer confirming prior telephone advice from FPPC staff (Mark Morodomi), not an official FPPC advice letter response. The document contains the lawyer's recitation of facts and the advice given orally, but lacks formal FPPC letterhead or official response structure. Document is seeking written confirmation of oral guidance rather than presenting a new question for FPPC response.

embedding.summary: Attorney Peter Bagatelos writes to confirm oral legal advice provided by FPPC Legal Division regarding the permissibility of using re-election committee funds for Bill Honig (California Superintendent of Public Instruction) to attend charitable events and give gifts to staff.
